questions.stem: A line passes through the points \((0, 4)\) and \((3, 10)\). Which of the following is the equation of the line?
questions.explanation:
Use the slope formula with the two given points \((0, 4)\) and \((3, 10)\).
$$m = \frac{10 - 4}{3 - 0} = \frac{6}{3} = 2$$
Since the line passes through \((0, 4)\), the \(y\)-intercept is \(4\). The equation of the line is \(y = 2x + 4\).

questions.stem: Which of the following values of \(x\) satisfies \(3x - 7 > 2\)?
questions.explanation:
Solve the inequality for \(x\).
$$\begin{aligned}3x - 7 &> 2 \\ 3x &> 9 \\ x &> 3\end{aligned}$$
The solution is \(x > 3\). Of the choices, only \(x = 4\) satisfies this condition. Note that \(x = 3\) does not work because the inequality is strict (\(>\), not \(\geq\)).

questions.stem: Which of the following is equivalent to \(x^2 + 6x + 5\) and reveals the minimum value of the expression?
questions.explanation:
Complete the square to rewrite the expression in vertex form.
$$\begin{aligned}x^2 + 6x + 5 &= x^2 + 6x + 9 - 9 + 5 \\ &= (x + 3)^2 - 4\end{aligned}$$
The expression \((x+3)^2 - 4\) reveals that the minimum value is \(-4\), occurring when \(x = -3\) (since \((x+3)^2 \geq 0\) for all real \(x\)).

questions.stem: In triangle \(ABC\), the measure of angle \(A\) is \(3x°\), the measure of angle \(B\) is \((2x + 20)°\), and the measure of angle \(C\) is \((x + 10)°\). What is the measure, in degrees, of angle \(B\)?
questions.explanation:
The sum of the angles in any triangle is \(180°\). Set up and solve an equation.
$$\begin{aligned}3x + (2x + 20) + (x + 10) &= 180 \\ 6x + 30 &= 180 \\ 6x &= 150 \\ x &= 25\end{aligned}$$
Substitute \(x = 25\) to find angle \(B\): \(2(25) + 20 = 70°\).

questions.stem: A jacket originally costs \$80. It is on sale for \$60. What is the percent decrease in price?
questions.explanation:
Percent decrease is calculated using the original price as the denominator.
$$\text{Percent decrease} = \frac{80 - 60}{80} \times 100 = \frac{20}{80} \times 100 = 25\%$$
A common error is dividing by the new price: \(\frac{20}{60} \approx 33\%\). Always use the original value when calculating percent change.

questions.stem: Line \(l\) is defined by the equation \(4x - 2y = 10\). Which of the following correctly describes line \(l\)?
questions.explanation:
Rewrite the equation in slope-intercept form \(y = mx + b\).
$$\begin{aligned}4x - 2y &= 10 \\ -2y &= -4x + 10 \\ y &= 2x - 5\end{aligned}$$
The slope is \(2\) and the \(y\)-intercept is \(-5\).

questions.stem: Which of the following is equivalent to \(\dfrac{x^3 - 2x^2 - 5x + 6}{x - 3}\), for \(x \neq 3\)?
questions.explanation:
Use synthetic division to divide \(x^3 - 2x^2 - 5x + 6\) by \(x - 3\).
$$\begin{array}{r|rrrr} 3 & 1 & -2 & -5 & 6 \\ & & 3 & 3 & -6 \\ \hline & 1 & 1 & -2 & 0 \end{array}$$
The remainder is \(0\), confirming \(x = 3\) is a root. The quotient is \(x^2 + x - 2\).

questions.stem: A farmer has only chickens and cows. There are 20 heads and 56 legs in total. How many cows does the farmer have?
questions.explanation:
Let \(c\) = number of cows and \(k\) = number of chickens. Each animal has 1 head; cows have 4 legs, chickens have 2.
$$\begin{aligned}c + k &= 20 \\ 4c + 2k &= 56\end{aligned}$$
From the first equation, \(k = 20 - c\). Substitute into the second equation.
$$\begin{aligned}4c + 2(20 - c) &= 56 \\ 4c + 40 - 2c &= 56 \\ 2c &= 16 \\ c &= 8\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: The functions \(f\) and \(g\) are defined by \(f(x) = 2x + 1\) and \(g(x) = x^2 - 3\). What is the value of \(f(g(2))\)?
questions.explanation:
First evaluate \(g(2)\), then substitute the result into \(f\).
$$\begin{aligned}g(2) &= (2)^2 - 3 = 4 - 3 = 1 \\ f(g(2)) &= f(1) = 2(1) + 1 = 3\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: The perimeter of a rectangle is given by \(P = 2l + 2w\), where \(l\) is the length and \(w\) is the width. Which of the following expresses \(l\) in terms of \(P\) and \(w\)?
questions.explanation:
Solve the formula for \(l\).
$$\begin{aligned}P &= 2l + 2w \\ P - 2w &= 2l \\ l &= \frac{P - 2w}{2}\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: A population of bacteria doubles every 3 hours. If there are initially 500 bacteria, which of the following functions gives the population \(P\) after \(t\) hours?
questions.explanation:
Since the population doubles every 3 hours, the exponent must equal 1 when \(t = 3\). This means the exponent is \(\frac{t}{3}\), giving \(P(t) = 500 \cdot 2^{t/3}\).
Check: at \(t = 0\), \(P = 500\) ✓. At \(t = 3\), \(P = 500 \cdot 2^1 = 1000\) ✓ (doubled). At \(t = 6\), \(P = 500 \cdot 2^2 = 2000\) ✓ (doubled again).

questions.stem: A circle has a radius of 6. A central angle measures \(120°\). What is the length of the arc intercepted by this angle?
questions.explanation:
Arc length is a fraction of the total circumference, determined by the central angle.
$$\text{Arc length} = \frac{120}{360} \times 2\pi(6) = \frac{1}{3} \times 12\pi = 4\pi$$

questions.stem: Line \(m\) has equation \(y = 3x - 2\). Line \(n\) is perpendicular to line \(m\) and passes through the point \((0, 1)\). What is the \(y\)-coordinate of the point on line \(n\) where \(x = 3\)?
questions.explanation:
The slope of a line perpendicular to line \(m\) is the negative reciprocal of \(3\), which is \(-\frac{1}{3}\). Line \(n\) passes through \((0, 1)\), so its equation is \(y = -\frac{1}{3}x + 1\).
$$y = -\frac{1}{3}(3) + 1 = -1 + 1 = 0$$

questions.stem: A recipe calls for 3 cups of flour for every 2 cups of sugar. If a baker uses 9 cups of flour, how many cups of sugar are needed?
questions.explanation:
Set up a proportion using the given ratio of flour to sugar.
$$\frac{3}{2} = \frac{9}{x} \implies 3x = 18 \implies x = 6$$

questions.stem: For what value of \(k\) does the equation \(kx^2 - 4x + 1 = 0\) have exactly one real solution?
questions.explanation:
A quadratic equation has exactly one real solution when its discriminant equals zero. For \(kx^2 - 4x + 1 = 0\), the discriminant is \(b^2 - 4ac = (-4)^2 - 4(k)(1) = 16 - 4k\).
$$16 - 4k = 0 \implies k = 4$$
When \(k = 4\), the equation \(4x^2 - 4x + 1 = 0\) factors as \((2x - 1)^2 = 0\), giving exactly one solution \(x = \frac{1}{2}\). ✓

questions.stem: Which of the following is equivalent to \((3x^2 - 2x + 5) - (x^2 + 4x - 3)\)?
questions.explanation:
Distribute the negative sign and combine like terms.
$$\begin{aligned}&(3x^2 - 2x + 5) - (x^2 + 4x - 3) \\ &= 3x^2 - 2x + 5 - x^2 - 4x + 3 \\ &= 2x^2 - 6x + 8\end{aligned}$$
A common error is forgetting to distribute the negative sign to all terms in the second polynomial, especially the \(-3\), which becomes \(+3\).

questions.stem: Ana and Ben together have \$120. Ana has \$30 more than Ben. How much money does Ben have?
questions.explanation:
Let \(a\) = Ana's amount and \(b\) = Ben's amount. Write a system of equations.
$$\begin{aligned}a + b &= 120 \\ a &= b + 30\end{aligned}$$
Substitute the second equation into the first.
$$\begin{aligned}(b + 30) + b &= 120 \\ 2b + 30 &= 120 \\ 2b &= 90 \\ b &= 45\end{aligned}$$

questions.stem: The function \(f\) is defined by \(f(x) = -(x - 2)^2 + 9\). What is the maximum value of \(f(x)\)?
questions.explanation:
The function is written in vertex form \(f(x) = a(x - h)^2 + k\), where the vertex is at \((h, k)\). Here \(a = -1\), \(h = 2\), and \(k = 9\).
Since \(a = -1 < 0\), the parabola opens downward, so the vertex represents a maximum. The maximum value of \(f(x)\) is \(\mathbf{9}\), occurring at \(x = 2\).

questions.stem: Which of the following represents all solutions to \(|x - 3| \leq 5\)?
questions.explanation:
For \(|x - 3| \leq 5\), rewrite as a compound inequality.
$$-5 \leq x - 3 \leq 5$$
Add 3 to all parts.
$$-2 \leq x \leq 8$$

questions.stem: In a \(30°\)-\(60°\)-\(90°\) triangle, the length of the shorter leg is 5. What is the length of the hypotenuse?
questions.explanation:
In a \(30°\)-\(60°\)-\(90°\) triangle, the side lengths are in the ratio \(1 : \sqrt{3} : 2\), where the shortest side is opposite the \(30°\) angle and the hypotenuse is twice the shorter leg.
$$\text{Hypotenuse} = 2 \times 5 = 10$$
Common errors: choosing \(5\sqrt{2}\) confuses this with a \(45°\)-\(45°\)-\(90°\) triangle, and \(5\sqrt{3}\) is the longer leg, not the hypotenuse.

questions.stem: A car travels at a constant speed. After 2 hours, the car has traveled 110 miles. After 5 hours, the car has traveled 275 miles. What is the car's speed in miles per hour?
questions.explanation:
Since speed is constant, use the slope formula to find the rate of change.
$$\text{Speed} = \frac{275 - 110}{5 - 2} = \frac{165}{3} = 55 \text{ mph}$$

questions.stem: A ball is thrown upward from a height of 4 feet. Its height \(h\), in feet, after \(t\) seconds is given by \(h(t) = -16t^2 + 48t + 4\). What is the maximum height, in feet, that the ball reaches?
questions.explanation:
The maximum height occurs at the vertex. Find the \(t\)-value of the vertex using \(t = -\frac{b}{2a}\).
$$t = -\frac{48}{2(-16)} = -\frac{48}{-32} = \frac{3}{2} = 1.5 \text{ seconds}$$
Substitute \(t = 1.5\) into \(h(t)\).
$$\begin{aligned}h(1.5) &= -16(1.5)^2 + 48(1.5) + 4 \\ &= -16(2.25) + 72 + 4 \\ &= -36 + 72 + 4 \\ &= 40\end{aligned}$$
